title: Persona switch mirrors a legacy trust-by-email session shortcut
impact_summary: Session trust shortcuts can accumulate through email suffixes, role strings, or client-side assumptions that were meant to be temporary.
why_this_matters: The boundary between a harmless demo shortcut and a real authz regression is something teams frequently underestimate.
evidence_fixture: disabled legacy admin session shortcut paired with safe local-only persona switching
suggested_patch_summary: Treat all client claims as untrusted and centralize session validation on the server.
verification_summary: Verified as safe because no real sessions or privileged claims are generated at runtime.
replay_regression_note: Compare the safe fake-session API output with the disabled legacy shortcut and explain the difference.
